description:
VP of Software Engineering
Canada, Remote

About CoLab

At CoLab, we help mechanical engineering teams bring life-changing products to market years sooner.

CoLab is the AI platform for driving stronger engineering decisions. Every design review in CoLab builds a knowledge repository of design feedback, decisions, and lessons learned - which AI agents draw from to flag issues on future designs before they compound. The more your team works in CoLab, the smarter it gets and the faster you arrive at the ideal design. Companies like Ford, Komatsu, and Johnson Controls use CoLab to catch issues earlier, eliminate rework cycles, and bring products to market faster.

Founded in St. John’s, Newfoundland, CoLab has grown quickly from our first customer in 2019 to a rapidly scaling company. We’ve recently been recognized on Deloitte’s Fast 50™ and Fast 500™, and named a Canadian company to watch by The Globe and Mail and Financial Post. 

About the Role

This role is for a high-impact engineering leader who has been in the trenches at fast-scaling startups — someone who has grown from individual contributor to leader and helped scale engineering orgs through multiple phases of growth. Ideally, you've done it more than once.

title: Engineering Manager
description:
Engineering Manager
Canada, Remote
Apply
About CoLab

At CoLab, we want to help mechanical engineering teams bring life-changing products to market years sooner.

CoLab is a cloud based platform for engineering design review. We make it easy for subject matter experts (SMEs) across your business to access, evaluate, and comment on 2D drawings and 3D models. Our built-in AI peer checker, AutoReview, scans designs for common errors or non-compliance with your standards and guidelines. AutoReview creates markups and comments on your files, in context – just like a human checker.

With CoLab, human SMEs and AI work together to help you make better decisions and improve designs faster. We automatically capture knowledge from across your global business that would otherwise be buried in emails, spreadsheets, slide decks, and unknown locations in Sharepoint or PLM. Then, we make sure every lesson learned and every design guideline is applied exactly when it matters.

Companies like Johnson Controls, Komatsu, Schaeffler, and Polaris have launched products 40% faster, cut BOM costs by 50%, and reduced quality escapes by 15% in 1 year.

About the Role

We’re hiring an Engineering Manager to lead Build Operator, one of the most strategically important product areas at CoLab.

Operator is our conversational AI agent for engineering teams. It helps users search engineering knowledge, understand complex design data, execute actions inside CoLab, and automate review workflows. 

A lot of th

Electrical Designers 

We’re growing our Design & Consulting team and looking for talented Electrical Designers to help shape the future of shipbuilding! 

 

As a Designer at Genoa, you’ll be at the forefront of major marine projects, transforming complex concepts into precise production designs and immersive 3D models. From powering innovative vessels to solving real-world engineering challenges, your work will directly support the creation of world-class ships.

 

Whether you’re launching your career or bringing years of expertise, you’ll collaborate with a passionate team, work with cutting-edge tools, and play a hands-on role in bringing ambitious designs to life with accuracy, creativity, and impact.

Piping Designers

 

We're seeking skilled and detail-oriented Piping Designers, of all levels, to join our Design & Consulting team in supporting innovative shipbuilding and marine engineering projects!

 

Our team is split into two main divisions that complement each other, 3D modeling and Manufacturing information (MI). The 3D model team takes engineering documents and turns them into a fully integrated digital ship. The MI team takes the 3D model and produces 2D drawings that the shipyard production facilities can fabricate from.

 

In this role, you’ll play a key part in developing piping systems and layouts for new vessel construction and refit programs. Working closely with other disciplines, production teams, and regulatory bodies, you’ll help bring complex designs to life using advanced 3D modeling tools and industry software to produce 2D drawings.

For more than 60 years, Northwood has been a powerful advocate for seniors and a leader in continuing care across Nova Scotia. Established as a social movement grounded in dignity, belonging, and choice, Northwood has grown into Atlantic Canada’s largest not for profit continuing care organization, serving more than 7,000 people through long-term care, home care, retirement living, and innovative community-based programs.

Northwood has been recognized for leading change in continuing care, advancing innovative models, embracing technology, and prioritizing dignity, choice, and belonging. Built on values of accountability, social justice, respect, and continuous improvement, Northwood remains deeply focused on people and ensuring individuals and families receive high-quality, compassionate care.

About Us

Propel is on a mission to build technology that strengthens the social safety net. We’re a passionate team of ~100 Propellers who envision a future where every American has the tools and resources they need to thrive.

The Role

Are you a Senior Fullstack Engineer looking to build 0→1 products at a mission-driven company? Do you enjoy creating consumer-grade experiences and thrive in high-autonomy startup environments?

We’re looking for a motivated Senior Fullstack Engineer to join Propel and help build new Medicare and Medicaid products from the ground up. In this role, you’ll work end-to-end across frontend and backend systems to develop user experiences that help people understand coverage options, surface savings opportunities, and enroll in healthcare plans.

title: Medicare Advisor
description:
Medicare Advisor
Location

USA; Brooklyn, NYC

Employment Type

Full time

Location Type

Remote

Department

New Ventures

OverviewApplication
About Us

Propel builds technology that strengthens the social safety net in America. We envision a future where every American has the tools and resources they need to thrive.

The safety net’s strength depends not just on funding, but delivery. Propel exists to dramatically improve how government benefits are delivered. Managing SNAP benefits, picking a D-SNP plan, or protecting against EBT theft should feel as seamless as Apple Pay or Netflix.

Governments struggle to build modern, scalable user experiences. We don’t.

Over 5.5 million Americans trust Propel today. We’ve grown revenue 35% YoY and are reinventing entire benefit categories, including Medicare, around the real needs of low-income Americans. We’re building a high-growth, high-ceiling, sustainable for-profit company where impact drives revenue.

About the Role

Propel serves approximately 1M users who are Medicare eligible, most of whom are also Medicaid eligible. We are building the first-ever app-based brokerage focused on helping "duals" (Americans on Medicare and Medicaid) enroll in healthcare plans. We specialize in Dual Eligible Special Needs Plans (D-SNPs) and are expanding to 30 states in 2026, with plans to move into larger Medicare markets over the next 24 months. As a Medicare Advisor, you will be a trusted expert, helping users compare Medicare plans, enroll

Base Salary $160K – $190K • Offers Equity
OverviewApplication

About Us

Propel is on a mission to empower low-income Americans by simplifying access to government benefits with modern technology. We're a passionate team of 100 Propellers - all working to help our users get through the month, every month.

About the Role

We're looking for a Solutions Architect to bridge Propel's Government Partnerships team and our Product & Engineering organization - translating state partners' needs into product strategy, and then owning the delivery of that work with states. This role has the opportunity to shape the way our product evolves with deeper government partnerships and increase the value we’re able to deliver to both users and government partners.

This is a hands-on IC role. You'll be the person writing the scoping doc, building the dashboard, sitting in the state meeting, configuring the campaign, and running the evaluation. You own both strategy and execution end to end. Over time, as this work scales, the role has potential to grow into a leadership position — but today we need someone who is excited about the execution.

You'll work across the state partnership funnel, from early relationship-building through campaign delivery. You should be the kind of person who can walk into a meeting with a state

description:

Founding Sales Lead
St. John’s, NL (Hybrid)

Help build the commercial engine of an AI-powered safety company with real product, real customers, and industrial pilots deploying this summer.

Audyse is building intelligent PPE that restores clear communication and real-time situational awareness in the world's loudest, most dangerous workplaces. We've shipped product to 16 countries, we're well-funded, and we're deploying to industrial pilot sites this summer across oil & gas, marine, manufacturing, and heavy industry.

We're looking for a Founding Sales Lead to help us build our sales motion from the ground up. This isn't cold-calling from a script, it's figuring out how to sell a category-defining product into industries where the buyers are safety managers, operations leads, and EHS directors.


‍

What You’ll Do

This is not a traditional BDR role, it’s a chance to help build the commercial engine of a deep-tech startup.

You will:

Lead sales outreach, qualification, and discovery with enterprise and mid-market industrial customers.
Run customer discovery to deeply understand problems, workflows, and buying processes.
Help shape our storytelling, messaging, and positioning based on real customer insights.
Work with the founding team to build and refine landing pages, funnels, and experiments to test what resonates.
Drive top-of-funnel pipeline, schedule demos, and support enterprise sales cycles.
Collaborate with marketing to grow ou

First firmware hire at an AI-powered safety company with a major oil & gas contract and industrial pilots launching this summer.

Audyse is building intelligent PPE that restores clear communication and real-time situational awareness in the world's loudest, most dangerous workplaces. Our headsets suppress harmful noise while preserving speech, alarms, and environmental cues, paired with a software platform that gives safety teams live exposure data and actionable insights.

We've already shipped 400 consumer headsets to 16 countries, completed 4 hardware revisions in 12 months, and navigated international manufacturing from Newfoundland. We've secured a major R&D contract and have 5 industrial pilots committed for this summer.

As our first dedicated firmware developer, you won't just write code, you'll own the firmware architecture, make critical decisions about reliability, connectivity, and power management, and ship software that performs in industrial conditions where failure isn't an option. You'll work directly with our CEO and CTO.



‍AI-Native Development

We're an AI-native team. You'll use Claude Code, Cursor, and Copilot daily to accelerate development, generating drivers, test harnesses, utilities, and boilerplate, then critically review and harden that code for production embedded systems. AI isn't a replacement for deep embedded engineering judgment. It's a multiplier.

First hardware hire at an AI-powered safety company with a major oil & gas contract and industrial pilots launching this summer.

Audyse is building intelligent PPE that restores clear communication and real-time situational awareness in the world's loudest, most dangerous workplaces. Design and iterate on schematics and PCB layouts for compact, industrial-grade IoT headset.

Collaborate with firmware, software, and product teams to ensure seamless integration.

At C-CORE, we are committed to investing in people and creating a dynamic and rewarding employment experience. If you are self-motivated, a team player, and committed to technical excellence, then C-CORE could be the right environment for you.

About C-CORE

C-CORE conducts R&D and provides research-based advisory services, delivering innovative engineering and technology solutions to national and international clients. Over the past 50 years, we have built a world-class team of 70 dedicated professionals with a reputation for excellence in Remote Sensing. We are looking for outstanding engineers and scientists to join our team.

Position Overview

This position requires an engineering degree with experience in CubeSat development, particularly in systems engineering tasks related to payload development, integration, testing and verification.

The successful candidate will have exceptional analysis and problem-solving skills, strong communication skills (spoken and written) and proven success working in a team environment.

Job description
What You’ll Do 
· Deliver and support a wide range of marine consultancy assignments for international clients across the offshore energy sector.
 
· Participate in preparing and reviewing FMEAs (Failure Modes and Effects Analyses) for DP systems, with a focus on modern DP2/DP3 vessels and emerging technologies.
 
· Support DP system assessments, including proving trials, ASOG reviews, capability analyses, and evaluations of vessel designs.
 
· Attend offshore vessels for trials, audits, and operational support.
 
· Participate in DP incident reviews, assist with root cause analysis, and contribute to the development of technical recommendations.
 
· Prepare clear, well structured technical reports, presentations, and evidence documentation including digital evidence packages to support assurance activities.
 
· Collaborate with internal teams and clients in a hybrid work environment, contributing to knowledge sharing, innovation, and continuous improvement.
 


What you'll gain
 
· Solid understanding of DP systems and operations.
 
· Experience on FMEA reports preparation, possibility to attend DP trials.

Compusult Limited is a dynamic and growing company headquartered in Mount Pearl, NL with Canadian branch offices in Halifax, NS; a U.S. subsidiary in Chantilly, VA; and a European office located in The Hague, Netherlands. We provide state-of-the-art software and systems for vital, mission-impacting technical environments, as well as associated services and support, to government and commercial entities, in particular the aerospace and defence industries. Compusult also designs and manufactures our own line of Unmanned Ground Vehicles (UGVs) and Assistive Technology products.

Compusult hires employees and subcontractors based primarily on required education and experience, and offers the same compensation to individuals and companies at the same levels of experience.

Compusult avoids unlawful discrimination in all aspects of employment, including recruitment and selection, promotion, transfer, opportunities for training, pay and benefits, other terms of employment, discipline, selection for redundancy and dismissal.

Bilingual Legal Assistant | Moncton, NB



Join a team dedicated to delivering outstanding client service.

Cox & Palmer is a full-service, top-ranked Atlantic Canadian law firm with the knowledge and experience clients rely upon for solid legal solutions. The firm prides itself on a collegial work atmosphere and supportive team environment.

We hire self-motivated, team-minded individuals with a strong work ethic, integrity and a commitment to client service.

We are seeking a permanent, full-time experienced Bilingual Legal Assistant to join our Moncton office to provide legal administrative support to lawyers in various practice groups.

SUMMARY OF RESPONSIBILITIES

Accurate electronic and physical maintenance of client and administrative files;
Draft legal documents and correspondence;
Verbal and written communication with clients;
Meet deadlines, ability to multi-task and prioritize incoming requests;
Proofread, data entry, and document production;
Establish and maintain a calendar and reminder system; and
Other related duties, performed with a high degree of detail and accuracy.
SKILLS & QUALIFICATIONS

Spoken and written proficiency in English and French is required;
Legal Assistant or Paralegal diploma from a recognized post-secondary institution is considered an asset;
Above average proficiency in Microsoft applications and typing skills; and

Wills & Estates Associate Lawyer | Halifax, NS



Join a team dedicated to delivering outstanding client service.

Cox & Palmer is a full-service, top-ranked Atlantic Canadian law firm with the knowledge and experience clients rely upon for solid legal solutions. The firm prides itself on a collegial work atmosphere and supportive team environment.

We hire self-motivated, team-minded individuals with a strong work ethic, integrity and a commitment to client service.

ABOUT COX & PALMER

200 lawyers strong, with over a century of experience serving clients. Cox & Palmer is a full-service regional firm providing advice to individuals and businesses in a broad range of sectors across all major industries.

We are committed to providing the highest quality of services to our clients. We are also committed to fostering an inclusive culture that values diverse perspectives and equitable opportunities.

 

THE OPPORTUNITY

Our Halifax office is excited to welcome an Associate Lawyer to our growing Wills & Estates practice group.  In this role, you will work closely with clients on a diverse range of matters, from drafting will and trusts to handling probate and estate litigation.  This practice group offers the opportunity to gain broader exposure to more sophisticated or high value estates including wealth transfers and complex multi-generational estate plan

Property Paralegal | Charlottetown, PE



Cox & Palmer is a full-service, top-ranked Atlantic Canadian law firm with the knowledge and experience clients rely upon for solid legal solutions. The firm prides itself on a collegial work atmosphere and supportive team environment.

We are seeking an experienced Property Paralegal to join our Charlottetown office. The ideal candidate will bring a strong background in residential real estate transactions, excellent organizational skills, and the ability to manage files in a fast-paced environment. While we value collaboration and ongoing development, this role is best suited for someone who can confidently support property transactions with minimal supervision.

SUMMARY OF RESPONSIBILITIES


Working as part of a collaborative team, you will:

Assist lawyers with all aspects of residential property files;
Draft documentation for property transactions;
Act and a liaison between the firm and clients, providing clear and timely communication on the status of transactions;
Manage ordering title searches and liaising with external partners (banks, other law firms, Realtors, IRAC and others);
SKILLS & QUALIFICATIONS

Minimum two years of experience working in residential real estate;
Completion of post-secondary studies is an asset;

Corporate Services Assistant
Corporate Services Assistant | Charlottetown, PE



Cox & Palmer is a full-service, top-ranked Atlantic Canadian law firm known for providing exceptional legal solutions. We pride ourselves on a collegial and supportive work environment and are seeking a Corporate Services Assistant with prior corporate legal experience to support our corporate team in Charlottetown.

Applicants must have at least three years of experience in corporate legal support or corporate administration. Candidates without relevant experience will not be considered.

SUMMARY OF RESPONSIBILITIES

Attend to all of the steps required to complete extra-provincial registrations, annual returns and name registrations for firm clients and for out-of-province clients and partner firms.
Draft or assist in drafting a wide range of legal documents and correspondence, ensuring high quality, accuracy and comprehensiveness, such as audit letters, annual returns, amendments, incorporations and related change notices, extra-provincial registrations, dissolutions, electronic filings, PPSA searches as well as maintenance of minute books.
Act as a liaison between lawyers, clients and other firms, as well as internal departments and external affiliates including government departments as requested.
Establish and maintain a current calendar of events and a deadline reminder system for assigned files.

At Digital Six, we don’t just staff projects, we lead transformation.

We are a boutique consulting firm based in Atlantic Canada with a track record of delivering complex, citizen-focused digital solutions across public sector and healthcare domains. From MyGovNL to One Person One Record, our consultants are behind some of the region’s most impactful digital initiatives.

We’re different, and we’re proud of it. We bring together experienced, intelligent, and ambitious individuals who are committed to developing a sphere of positive influence with our clients and in our communities.

The Opportunity

We are seeking a Senior Project Manager to lead key digital and technology initiatives with our public sector clients. You will manage complex, multi-stakeholder projects and ensure delivery aligns with expectations, budget, and timelines.

You will work as part of an agile, collaborative team that believes in communication, quality, and knowledge sharing—bringing structure, clarity, and calm to challenging and high-stakes engagements.

Plan and deliver IM/IT projects from initiation through implementation
Develop project charters, work breakdown structures, schedules, and

title: IT & Infrastructure Specialist
description:
Privacy Policy
Job Openings
IT & Infrastructure Specialist
Administration - St. John's, Newfoundland and Labrador

Job Posting: IT & Infrastructure Specialist

About the Role 

This is a new role on our team. As we grow, we need someone to take ownership of the operational layer so the rest of the team can stay focused on building. You’ll make sure our tools, hardware, infrastructure, and office run smoothly day-to-day.

IT is the foundation of this role, but there’s real opportunity to contribute to development and DevOps work alongside the engineering team if that’s where your interests lie. If you’re an IT person who can code, or a developer who enjoys the operations side, read on.

 

What You’ll Do

 

People & Workplace Operations

Run onboarding and offboarding from an IT perspective: accounts, devices, and access, end-to-end
Manage software licensing and costs across the company
Own everything in our physical space: networking, hardware procurement, a standardized desk setup for every employee, and physical access control
Manage vendor relationships with ISPs, equipment suppliers, and hardware warranty providers
Keep a clean, well-documented inventory so we always know what we have and what we need
Build the runbooks so everything is consistent and auditable as we grow

 

Infrastructure & Compliance

Manage the test systems our dev team uses in the office, including the 3rd party systems we integrate with
Manage our website infrastructure
Device management and securi

title: Product Designer
description:
Privacy Policy
Job Openings
Product Designer
Product - St. John's, Newfoundland and Labrador

Job Posting: Product Designer

Enaimco’s mission is to give heavy industry the ability to manage and understand their assets remotely — starting with subsea, one of the world’s toughest environments. Our customers run critical offshore operations daily, and they rely on us to make complex workflows simple, reliable, and intuitive.

We’re looking for a Product Designer to help shape the user experience and visual design of our platform. You’ll work directly with developers, engineers, and customers to turn complex workflows into clear, powerful tools that solve real operational problems.




What You’ll Do

Design workflows and interfaces that are core to our product experience.
Work closely with users to understand their challenges, test ideas, and refine solutions through feedback and iteration.
Collaborate directly with developers, product management, and other team members on a daily basis. We communicate heavily and work closely together to solve problems early.
Apply your skills to a unique product space — including 3D virtual environments and other novel interaction models designed to improve operational clarity and efficiency.
Contribute to the evolution of Enaimco’s design system to improve consistency and scalability across the platform.
Create wireframes, prototypes, and polished UI designs that balance usability, clarity, and speed.
Help foster a strong user-focused cultur

🌍 Women's Health Sales & Business Development Lead
📍 Atlantic Canada | Full-Time | Contract
🗓 Application Deadline: April 10, 2026

About Granville Biomedical
Granville Biomedical is a Canadian health tech company dedicated to advancing women’s pelvic and reproductive health through the design and commercialization of innovative anatomical models and educational tools. Our mission is to empower clinicians, educators, and patients globally with products that promote health equity, evidence-based care, and inclusive design.

Over the past year, we have made significant progress by expanding internationally, launching new proprietary products, and building strategic partnerships in women’s health. We are now seeking a highly motivated and self-driven Women's Health Sales & Business Development Lead to help amplify our global impact and take our growth to the next level.

This is a full-time 6-month contract position with an opportunity for extension. Based on funding requirements, preference will be given to candidates based in Atlantic Canada.

💼 The Role
The ideal candidate will hail from a sales or account manager background with an interest in marketing and experience working within a collaborative team environment. The candidate will lead and execute Granville Biomedical’s sales and business development strategy, focusing on growing international sales and developing key partners

title: Managing Director
description:
Skip to content
Home
Our Team
Blog
FAQ
Contact
709-739-1953
OUR SERVICES
RECRUITMENT
HR CONSULTING
FRACTIONAL HR
OPEN POSITIONS
Managing Director

Higher Talent has partnered with Pilot° in their search for a Managing Director reporting to their St. John’s, NL office.

ABOUT THE ROLE: 

Reporting to the President & Executive Chair, the Managing Director (MD) is the senior operational and leadership executive responsible for the day-to-day performance, stability, and growth of Pilot°. The MD translates vision into execution, ensuring the agency operates profitably, efficiently, and with strategic clarity.

This role is ideal for a seasoned leader who combines business acumen, people leadership, and operational discipline, and who thrives in a creative, client-driven environment. The MD must be a confident, credible leader internally and with senior clients.

The Managing Director is central to Pilot’s next chapter—ensuring the agency is strong, stable, scalable, and future-ready, while preserving the values, reputation, and creative excellence that define the brand for more than three decades.

Chief Administrative Officer

Higher Talent is excited to conduct an expedited search for a motivated and community‑focused

 Chief Administrative Officer (CAO) for the Town of Witless Bay, Newfoundland and Labrador.

WHO WE ARE: 

The Town of Witless Bay is not just a great place to visit but is also a great community to live and do business.  Located on Route 10 as a part of The Irish Loop, Witless Bay is a small, scenic, traditional Newfoundland outport community with a population of 1640 that offers an abundance of Natural Tourism. Hiking, camping, and fishing can be found along the East Coast Trail. Sea kayaking is very popular in the Witless Bay area. For the amateur looking for adventure, there are several businesses offering boat and sea kayaking tours in the area. Icebergs, whales, and incredible bird colonies are just some of the attractions this scenic area has to offer. The Witless Bay Ecological Reserve is a must see! To learn more about us, please click on the Town of Witless Bay.

THE ROLE: 

Reporting to the Town Council, we are seeking a motivated and community‑focused Chief Administrative Officer (CAO) which is a senior management position responsible for the strategic leadership and efficient delivery of all the Town’s administrative and operational services. The CAO provides highly effective advice, guidance and support to the May

We’re innovative, progressive, and growing fast, redefining what it means to be an independent insurance broker in Atlantic Canada. As one of the region’s largest and most dynamic brokerages, Munn Insurance combines the stability of 120 years in business with the energy and vision of a digital-first organization.

We’re proud of our reputation for exceptional service, innovative solutions, and community involvement. Competitive, personalized, and customer-obsessed.  We’re not your average insurance broker!

This is a key role for someone who thrives on building meaningful partnerships and wants to help shape the future of a rapidly expanding organization.

We Are Hiring!

We are seeking a highly organized, detail-oriented Administrative Assistant to provide administrative, financial, social media and membership support to the organization. As part of our small team, you’ll have an amazing opportunity to contribute to the growth and advancement of our ocean industry – provincially, nationally, and globally. 

 Key Responsibilities: 

Provide general administrative support, scheduling meetings, and maintaining records. 
Assist with financial administration, including processing invoices, expense reports, and maintaining financial records. 
Support budget tracking, financial reporting, and reconciliation of accounts. 
Assist in preparing member newsletter, reports, presentations, and correspondence. 
Liaise with vendors, partners, and stakeholders as needed. 
Maintain and update databases, filing systems, and organizational records. 
Provide support for event planning, logistics, and coordination. 
Manage and update social media accounts and website. 
Support membership services by maintaining records, processing renewals, and responding to member inquiries. 
Other duties as assigned to support the team.

Benefits: Onboarding

Position Details

Forestry, Agriculture and Lands

This position is responsible for providing administrative support to staff. The incumbent is responsible for: serving the public by phone, email or in person; issuing various permits; preparation of correspondence and reports; maintenance of office records; data entry into Oracle FMS and HPE Records Manager; managing the office mail; drafting response letters for signature; answering incoming routine calls and independently handling complaints and requests for information; scheduling and assisting with meetings and presentations; coordinating the compilation and distribution of reports; ordering and managing inventory of office supplies; and performing other related duties as required.

Diploma in Business or Office Administration (equivalencies will be considered)
Experience in administrative practices
Experience with information management applications

About Us

Propel is on a mission to empower low-income Americans by simplifying access to government benefits with modern technology. We're a passionate team of 100 Propellers - all working to help our users get through the month, every month. 

The Role

We launched the Propel Marketplace to connect our ~5M monthly active users to products, services, and exclusive offers from leading brands across grocery and retail, healthcare, telecoms, financial services, and auto. It has grown into a powerful, industry-leading performance tool for our partners and a critical revenue driver for Propel. We have strong product market fit, our users love our app, and our customers love that we have a significant reach and engagement with our users. 

We’re looking for an Insights & Strategy Lead to own our Car Insurance and Financial Services verticals. 

Here's how you'll impact the company

Drive account revenue growth through data-driven upselling and optimisation: Partner closely with Customer Success to identify and quantify high-impact upsell opportunities, build compelling data-driven sales materials, and maintain critical business intelligence on key accounts to inform expansion strategies 

AI fluency: You proactively explore ways to use AI tools to deepen your analysis and improve team workflows (for example, using Claude or Gem

About Us

Propel is on a mission to build technology that strengthens the social safety net. We’re a passionate team of ~100 Propellers who envision a future where every American has the tools and resources they need to thrive.

The Role

Are you a Platform Engineer looking to make a big impact on a mission-driven company? Do you enjoy architecting software that shapes an entire engineering organization? Do you thrive in high-autonomy startup environments?

We’re looking for a self-motivated Senior Platform Engineer to join our Developer Enablement Team within the engineering org at Propel.

This role empowers other engineers by improving the cloud native platforms that enable development and delivery of Propel’s technology. These cloud native platforms impact several products and stacks, including interacting with government programs, surfacing savings opportunities, enrolling users in healthcare plans, internal tooling, architecture, and more! Your focus will be on running fault tolerant applications in Kubernetes and AWS, and solving organization-wide engineering problems. 

We are looking for a tinkerer to experiment with improvements to the developer experience at Propel. You will architect new features for our cloud platform, support a wide-variety of production applications, address technical debt, and help guide other

Junior or Intermediate Biologist

SEM is currently seeking a Junior or Intermediate Biologist for a three month contract, from June 1 to September 1, to be based out of our St. John’s office. This contract will have a possibility of extension.  

The biologist will assist the SEM team in completing project-based aquatics work for a variety of clients across Newfoundland and Labrador. 

Responsibilities 

•    Assisting with various freshwater aquatic surveys  
•    Data analysis, interpretation of technical reporting and report preparation.

•    Utilizing various field instruments for the collection of data while maintaining accurate records as per company quality assurance/quality control protocols.
•    Training will be provided for specialized data collection tasks.

•    Post-secondary education in Biology or a related field of study 
•    A passion for aquatic biology and the outdoors
•    Valid driver’s license
•    Willingness to travel extensively around the province during this contract

description:

Get In Touch
Get In Touch
Construction Management Representatives
On behalf of our client, SEM requires the services of up to four individuals to join its engineering and project management team to act as Construction Management Representatives (CMRs) to support the execution of an upcoming project regarding the remediation of access bridges along the Labrador Island Link (LIL) Transmission Line.

The work will require significant travel to remote areas and is expected to occur throughout the entire LIL Asset located between Soldier’s Pond and Muskrat Falls and the successful candidates may be required to work either in Labrador or Newfoundland. 

Additional support may be required for other projects in and/or around Churchill Falls, Muskrat Falls, Soldiers Pond and Holyrood. 
 
Key Responsibilities

Project Oversight & Coordination

Act as the primary on-site liaison between the Project Manager and contractors
Contribute to daily coordination meetings with operations staff and contractors
Coordinate work activities, outages, and site access with operations and other trades
Communicate scope changes, risks, and issues to the Project Manager

Monitoring & Reporting

Monitor contractor activities to ensure compliance with schedule, scope, and technical specifications
Maintain daily progress reports, including updates, issues

Manager of Fund Development & Communications

 
Posted: 05/07/2026  Executive and Management

About Us
Award-winning. Inspiring. Values-driven. Entrepreneurial spirit. These are some of the words that describe Stella’s Circle, a leading social justice organization that works with over 1,500 people each year, championing growth and transformation through housing, mental health, and employment.
 
The Position
The Manager of Fund Development and Communications is responsible for the planning, coordination, and oversight of fund development and communications functions to support organizational sustainability, brand integrity, and stakeholder engagement. The role oversees fundraising activities, communications strategy, media relations, and team operations to ensure consistent, aligned, and effective messaging across the organization. As a member of the Management Team, the Manager contributes to organizational planning, cross-functional coordination, and advancement of strategic priorities, ensuring that communications and fundraising efforts support program delivery, organizational visibility, and long-term growth.
What You’ll Do
Fund Development & Donor Relations

Leads the development and implementation of a comprehensive fund development strategy, including individual giving, corporate partnerships, foundation funding, and government opportunities, to support organizational revenue targets

title: Senior Programmer
description:
HOW IT WORKS
 
ABOUT US
 
RESOURCES
 
CONTACT US
 
SUPPORT PORTAL
SENIOR PROGRAMMER

Do you dream of having a say in the software you write? Strong Data Automation is an old company with a start up mentality that currently specializes in waste management logistics. That’s garbage you say? Exactly! We are a tight team working to build essential, usable products for our customers quickly and everyone pitches in to get the job done right. You’ll work on problems of secure data collection, operation scaling and automation, and hardware integrations across multiple applications and geographical locations. Customers will love you for making their job about high level decision making instead of copy and pasting between spreadsheets. We handle tracking, billing, trending and more as we try to revolutionize how organizations control what happens to everything the world doesn’t want.

ROLE REQUIREMENTS
5+ years programming experience
 
Computer science degree or equivalent experience / education
 
Full stack programming experience working primarily in the .NET ecosystem
 
Familiar with modern development best practices and design patterns
 
Knowledge of key security fundamentals for web application development
 
Strong knowledge of SQL Server database
 
Thrives in an atmosphere of taking on new challenges and developing new skills

Strong Data is a software company whose goal is to create great software that allows its customers to work as efficiently as possible. Secon

Customer Service Representative

Have you ever noticed how some jobs don’t feel like work? We do. That’s why we go out of our way to make sure our employees love coming to work every day. For starters, they get to take their birthdays off…with pay. Sound good? There’s a lot more! If you would like to join our dynamic team, we currently have three Customer Service Positions available for our growing team.

The ideal candidate will deliver excellence in customer service when answering the phones for our clients using proper telephone etiquette and will embrace Telelink’s core values. You have the ability to work evening and weekend shifts; strong knowledge of internet navigation; typing speed of 30 wpm would be an asset; strong interpersonal and communication skills and the ability to work effectively with a wide range of constituencies in a diverse company. Knowledge of Microsoft Office applications would be an asset.

Sales Executive
Working With Telelink

Telelink is a 60+ year old start-up. Our history is in the traditional answering service industry. Our future is one that bridges humans and technology to deliver connected customer experiences.  

As the Sales Executive, you are the first impression, the problem solver, and the gas that fuels our revenue engine. Your primary responsibilities include qualifying inbound leads, progressing qualified opportunities all the way through contract negotiations using a consultative selling approach, hunting for strategic customers in our target markets, and advising potential customers on the right solutions that deliver their desired outcomes. As the first point of contact on the customer journey, you set the tone for Telelink’s customer experience by delving into the customer’s desired outcomes, and ensuring these and all details required to deliver on those outcomes are fully documented and cascaded to other teams along the customer journey.

General Manager

Venor is proud to partner with Terra Nova Landscaping in their search for a General Manager to join their team in Bedford, Nova Scotia.

Terra Nova Landscaping is a growing landscaping and construction services company known for its hands-on approach, strong industry relationships, and commitment to delivering high-quality work across the Halifax Regional Municipality. Supporting a variety of landscaping, construction, and site-related projects, the company has built a strong reputation through reliable execution and long-standing client partnerships.

The General Manager will oversee day-to-day operations while supporting business development and project execution across the region. Reporting directly to ownership, this role is ideal for a hands-on leader with strong industry connections, operational experience, and the ability to help drive continued growth across the business.

What You’ll Be Doing
In this role, you will lead operations across field teams, projects, and business development initiatives while ensuring projects are delivered safely, efficiently, and profitably.

PERMANENT · ADMINISTRATIVE & OFFICE SUPPORT
Executive Assistant

Venor is proud to partner with the College of Physicians & Surgeons of Nova Scotia (CPSNS) to recruit an Executive Assistant.

CPSNS serves and protects the public by effectively regulating the province’s medical profession in accordance with the Medical Act and its regulations. CPSNS is a culture of listening – listening to the public and the profession. The College prides itself in ensuring an environment that is respectful, safe and humble. CPSNS values engaging with Nova Scotians and the communities served across the province. Serving the public, the College ensures Nova Scotia’s physicians and surgeons are qualified, competent and fit to practice medicine to provide the best care possible to patients.

 

What you’ll do

The Executive Assistant provides confidential, efficient, and comprehensive administrative support to the Registrar & CEO and Deputy Registrar. This role serves as a central point of coordination for executive operations, including managing schedules and meetings, preparing correspondence and reports, and handling travel and event logistics. Senior Cyber Security Consultant

Venor is proud to partner with IMP Solutions on their search for a Senior Cyber Security Consultant to join their team.

IMP Solutions, a division of IMP Group Limited, provides IT products, IT services, and business consulting solutions to clients across Canada. Their team is built on integrity, passion, mutual respect, and a shared commitment to service excellence. With a growing Cybersecurity & Risk practice, IMP Solutions offers the opportunity to work on impactful client engagements that support business transformation, resilience, and innovation.

The Senior Cyber Security Consultant will act as a trusted advisor to clients, leading and supporting engagements focused on cybersecurity maturity, risk, governance, and resilience. This role is well suited for a seasoned cybersecurity practitioner who combines strong technical and risk assessment expertise with the ability to facilitate workshops, engage executive stakeholders, and translate complex findings into clear, business-focused recommendations.

We’re growing our strong, cohesive, aligned team with a can-do attitude, that’s driven by our values. We foster an inclusive culture and it shows - staff turnover is lower than industry average. We celebrate wins, and take care of each other.

title: Account Executive, ERP Solutions US Midwest Region
description:
Privacy Policy
Job Openings
Account Executive, ERP Solutions US Midwest Region
Sales - United States (Remote)

Account Executive, ERP – Midwest US 

Permanent Full Time 
Location: Remote - Midwest US (open to applicants from IL, IA, MI, MN, MO, OH, WI)




Grow Your Earnings. Close Lucrative Deals. Build Your Career.

If you’re a motivated Account Executive who wants your earnings to reflect your performance, Vision33 has an opportunity for you to build a highly rewarding sales career.

 

At Vision33, we are looking for a driven Account Executive in Midwest, US, who is motivated by building relationships, closing net-new business, and significantly growing their income through performance. 




High performers thrive here. With our uncapped commission potential, strong sales support, and a proven delivery organization, Account Executives can substantially and consistently grow their income year over year.







The Role

As an Account Executive, you will take ownership of the full sales cycle for net-new ERP software opportunities, developing and executing strategies that generate pipeline, win new clients, and exceed revenue targets.

You will:

Drive net-new revenue growth by developing and managing a strong pipeline of qualified opportunities.
Prospect and engage new clients, building relationships with decision-makers from operations leaders to C-suite executives.
Forecast demand and manage your pipeline to consistently exceed monthly and annual sales targets.
Position

This role also includes performance-based incentives tied to key business objectives.




As part of this strategic team, you’ll partner closely with both sales and delivery teams — helping shape solution strategy during pre-sales engagements, leading compelling demonstrations, and supporting successful customer outcomes. You’ll work alongside experienced leaders in a collaborative, fast-moving environment where storytelling, innovation, and a cloud-first mindset are highly valued.

Vision33 est une firme mondiale de services de conseils professionnels en informatique qui résout les défis commerciaux des clients grâce à des services de conseils transformationnels et des solutions technologiques. Nous travaillons en partenariat avec des organisations en croissance dans les secteurs public et privé pour comprendre leur activité et les aider à réaliser leur vision avec la bonne combinaison de stratégie, de conseil et de technologie. Les membres de l’équipe hautement qualifiés et axés sur les résultats de Vision33 fournissent une expertise transparente en matière de processus commerciaux et de technologie dans divers secteurs et groupes de clients. 


 

Vision33 Canada Inc., un partenaire primé de Sage Intacct, a été nommé gagnant du Sage Platinum Club pour l'exercice 2023. 
 
Vision33 est actuellement à la recherche d'un consultant en implantation de Sage Intacct (bilingue) pour rejoindre notre équipe. Si vous recherchez une opportunité passionnante, nous avons un rôle pour vous! 
 
Dans ce rôle pratique, vous serez responsable de : 

Fournir des services de conseil, de la formation et de l'assistance pour assurer aux clients des déploiements réussis de solutions logicielles de comptabilité en infon

title: Cloud IT Consultant
description:
Privacy Policy
Job Openings
Cloud IT Consultant
Cloud - Calgary, Alberta (Remote)

Cloud IT Consultant, Cloud AWS Services
Full Time / Permanent





Ready to take cloud deployments to the next level? Join our fast-growing team as an AWS Cloud IT Consultant, where you’ll design, deploy, and optimize AWS environments for SAP Business One and SAGE clients.




You’ll work hands-on with Linux and Windows servers, manage web and application services, and support client go-lives—even after hours. Your expertise will help shape scalable, secure cloud systems that drive real business results.




What You’ll Do:

Deploy AWS environments for SAP & SAGE clients.
Configure and support web and application services (IIS, Apache, Nginx) and file/print services.
Lead go-live migrations, including after-hour cutovers.
Document build processes, adhere to policies, develop and maintain procedural documentation, and support continuous improvement.
Collaborate with clients, consultants, and stakeholders to ensure successful builds and deployments.
Monitor system and database backups to ensure processes are running as expected.
Complete environmental restorations and server maintenance.
Respond to security events and resolve issues. 
Identify potential issues and drive proactive solutions.





Experience Required for the Role:

5+ years’ AWS or other cloud deployment.
Strong Linux skills (SUSE preferred) and solid Windows/AD/Networking knowledge (VPN, Firewalls, AWS Security groups).
Deployment

Cloud Experience Manager 

Full Time/Permanent

Make our cloud delivery feel seamless, confident, and controlled.  

  

As Cloud Experience Manager, you will own the end-to-end client experience for cloud service delivery — from quoting and implementation through go-live and support transition. This role is equal parts coordination, leadership, and customer advocacy – including both delivery leadership and operational ownership. 

 

Headquartered in St. John’s, NL, you’ll lead a delivery team and collaborate across sales, engineering, delivery, and operations to support clients across North America, the United Kingdom, and global locations. 

 

The Role  

You’ll sit at the center of cloud delivery execution, service operations, and customer experience — driving alignment across teams and acting as the primary owner of the delivery experience. Your focus is ensuring cloud solutions are delivered smoothly, meet commercial and operational expectations, and transition successfully into live production with minimal friction.  

 

Key Responsibilities  

Own the end‑to‑end cloud delivery and go‑live experience, ensuring smooth and predictable customer launches.  
Partner with sales to align delivery timelines, scope, and customer expectations.

Support clients through training, testing, go-live readine

DP FMEA Consultant / Electrical Engineer
Marine Service · Gdynia · Permanent · On site
Share job

Job description
What You’ll Do 
· Deliver and support a wide range of marine consultancy assignments for international clients across the offshore energy sector.
 
· Participate in preparing and reviewing FMEAs (Failure Modes and Effects Analyses) for DP systems, with a focus on modern DP2/DP3 vessels and emerging technologies.
 
· Support DP system assessments, including proving trials, ASOG reviews, capability analyses, and evaluations of vessel designs.
 
· Attend offshore vessels for trials, audits, and operational support.
 
· Participate in DP incident reviews, assist with root cause analysis, and contribute to the development of technical recommendations.
 
· Prepare clear, well structured technical reports, presentations, and evidence documentation including digital evidence packages to support assurance activities.
 
· Collaborate with internal teams and clients in a hybrid work environment, contributing to knowledge sharing, innovation, and continuous improvement.
 


What you'll gain
 
· Solid understanding of DP systems and operations.
 
· Experience on FMEA reports preparation, possibility to attend DP trials.

Senior Performance Marketing Manager

About CoLab

At CoLab, we help mechanical engineering teams bring life-changing products to market years sooner.

CoLab is the AI platform for driving stronger engineering decisions. Every design review in CoLab builds a knowledge repository of design feedback, decisions, and lessons learned - which AI agents draw from to flag issues on future designs before they compound. The more your team works in CoLab, the smarter it gets and the faster you arrive at the ideal design. Companies like Ford, Komatsu, and Johnson Controls use CoLab to catch issues earlier, eliminate rework cycles, and bring products to market faster.

Founded in St. John’s, Newfoundland, CoLab has grown quickly from our first customer in 2019 to a rapidly scaling company. We’ve recently been recognized on Deloitte’s Fast 50™ and Fast 500™, and named a Canadian company to watch by The Globe and Mail and Financial Post. 

About the Role

This role owns paid distribution at CoLab. You’ll own the performance, growth, and evolution of our paid media program — with LinkedIn as the primary channel, but not the only one. Your job is to take what’s already working, improve it month over month, and expand into new channels that help us reach the right buyers in new ways.

That means running and scaling LinkedIn paid social, owning our paid newsletter program, and designing thoughtful experiments in emerging or underutilized channels (e.g., Reddit, YouTube, or o

Founded in St. John’s, Newfoundland, CoLab has grown quickly from our first customer in 2019 to a rapidly scaling company. We’ve recently been recognized on Deloitte’s Fast 50™ and Fast 500™, and named a Canadian company to watch by The Globe and Mail and Financial Post.

About the Role

We’re hiring a Senior Product Manager to lead one of the most ambitious bets at CoLab: building AI-powered review systems that help engineering teams identify issues before they become expensive downstream problems.

This team owns AutoReview — the capabilities that proactively surface risks, patterns, missing context, and engineering concerns across complex design workflows. The goal isn’t to replace engineers. It’s to help them make better decisions, faster, using the collective knowledge generated acro

Litigation Paralegal | Halifax, NS



Join a team dedicated to delivering outstanding client service.

Cox & Palmer is a full-service, top-ranked Atlantic Canadian law firm with the knowledge and experience clients rely upon for solid legal solutions. The firm prides itself on a collegial work atmosphere and supportive team environment.

Position Description:
This is an exciting time for CGI, and we want you to be part of it. We are Canada’s largest
independent information technology services firm, and we are still growing! We are expanding
in Atlantic Canada, and we need your skills, enthusiasm, and dedication as part of our team.

Your future duties and responsibilities:

Elicit, analyze, and document business and technical requirements, including data mapping, process flows, and use cases.
Develop deep understanding of application architecture, system interdependencies, payment flows, and configurations.
Analyze and manipulate large datasets while identifying process improvements and ensuring strong controls.
Act as a liaison and leader between business stakeholders, technology teams, and external vendors at both project and program levels.
Contribute to project planning by reviewing requirements, data models, and documentation; support scope, timelines, and deliverables.
Support system design, security, standards, and quality metrics while ensuring integration and reusability of solutions.
Lead testing activities, implementation, conversion strategies, and production readiness; conduct pre- and post-implementation reviews.
Provide user documentation, training, post-implementation support, and ensure business continuity and recovery plans.

SENIOR WEB DEVELOPER, REACT

Position Description:
We are seeking a Senior Web Developer with strong expertise in React to join our CGI offices in Atlantic Canada. This is a full-time, permanent position requiring 10+ years of experience in software development. In this role, you will design, develop, and deliver modern, scalable web applications while contributing across the full software development lifecycle.

This is a permanent fulltime, hybrid position requiring 2-3 days onsite at our Atlantic Canada offices- Halifax, NS; Moncton, NB; Fredericton, NB; Stratford, PEI or St. John’s, NL.

Your future duties and responsibilities:

Design, develop, and maintain modern web applications using React and other JavaScript frameworks
Build scalable and maintainable front-end architectures, including single-page applications (SPAs)
Develop responsive, accessible, and high-quality user interfaces using modern JavaScript (ES6+) and CSS techniques
Collaborate closely with Product, Design, and engineering teams to deliver innovative and user-centric features
Integrate front-end applications with backend systems and APIs using GraphQL
Debug and optimize applications across modern browsers including Chrome, Safari, and mobile browsers

 Job Posts Project Manager

PROJECT MANAGER

Position Description:
We are seeking an experienced Intermediate to Senior Project Manager to lead the planning, execution, and delivery of complex projects across business, technology, and operational environments. The successful candidate will manage scope, schedule, budget, risks, stakeholders, vendors, and delivery teams while ensuring strong client engagement and high-quality project outcomes.

This role is well suited for a project manager who can operate independently, communicate confidently with senior stakeholders and clients, and bring structure, discipline, and momentum to cross-functional initiatives.

Your future duties and responsibilities:

Manage end-to-end project delivery, including initiation, planning, execution, monitoring, control, and closure.
Define project scope, objectives, deliverables, success criteria, timelines, budgets, and resource plans.
Serve as a primary point of contact for clients, maintaining strong working relationships and ensuring clear communication throughout the project lifecycle.
Lead client-facing meetings, project status updates, steering committees, workshops, and governance forums.
Translate client needs and business objectives into actionable project plans, deliverables, and delivery priorities.
Manage client expectations related to scope, timelines, budget, risks, issues, dependencies, and change r

 Job Posts Sr. Product Manager

SR. PRODUCT MANAGER

About Us
The only tool you need to outsmart social 📈

Dash Social is a social media management platform that equips brands with intelligence and speed to stay ahead of the curve. Through its sophisticated cross-channel insights and workflow tools, Dash Social enables brands to create content that entertains, engages and drives consistent business results. To discover how Dash Social is empowering brands to outsmart social, visit dashsocial.com.

At Dash Social, you will be given the opportunity to take risks, learn, and grow your career. You’ll be joining a talented, hardworking team with leading policies and practices recognized as a top employer in Canada.

As a Sr. Product Manager, you will own the end-to-end strategy and roadmap for a dedicated product or customer journey and provide formal leadership to a cross-functional development team. This includes defining the product vision and strategy for your defined area of expertise.

You’ll also help define how AI shows up across your product pillar – from copilots that accelerate customer workflows to agentic features that handle work autonomously – and you’ll use AI tooling yourself to move faster on research, specs, and analysis.

PROJECT MANAGER/SCRUM MASTER

About the role
We are seeking a highly qualified Project Manager/Scrum master with 8+ years of experience managing end-to-end project lifecycle, ensuring timely delivery and alignment with business objectives. As a Project Manager/Scrum Master, you will play a pivotal role in designing, developing, and maintaining high-quality software solutions. This role offers the opportunity to work in a dynamic environment, contributing to impactful projects that align with the company’s vision.

In this role, you will:

Coordinate Agile ceremonies, including sprint planning, daily stand-ups, and retrospectives, to maintain team focus and productivity.
Utilize Atlassian Confluence and Jira to track project progress, manage tasks, and ensure transparency across stakeholders.
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to identify project requirements and establish clear goals.
Monitor project risks and implement mitigation strategies to ensure smooth execution – Provide regular updates to stakeholders, ensuring alignment and addressing any concerns promptly.
Drive continuous improvement within the team by analyzing past performance and implementing actionable insights.

CAREERS
Join the Team Building Software for Critical Operations

Focus FS partners with operators in energy, mining, and heavy industry to digitize the workflows their teams depend on. The platforms we build run in some of the world's most demanding environments where reliability, safety, and speed are not optional.

We're hiring across sales, engineering, and solutions roles. If you want to do meaningful work alongside industry leaders and help shape what operational intelligence looks like for the next generation of industrial work, we'd like to hear from you.

Career Opportunities

Business Development · Flexible
Industrial Mid-Market Account Executive

New business across heavy industrial, mining, construction, and oil & gas.

Read More →

Enterprise Account Executive

Strategic enterprise accounts in mining and oil & gas.

Read More →

Solution Engineer

Technical lead translating customer challenges into solutions.

Read More →
Design · St. John's, NL
Product Designer (UX/UI)

End-to-end UX/UI ownership from requirements to build-ready designs.

Read More →

Full Stack Developer

Build and ship production SaaS applications end-to-end.

Read More →

SPANL was founded in September of 1987 by Margaret Acreman. Through a comprehensive range of programs and services—including counseling, education, childcare, emergency assistance, and community supports—SPANL fosters self-sufficiency and resilience in families across the province.

title: Senior Firmware Developer
description:
Welcome to the Kraken Robotics Career Center

Senior Firmware Developer

Senior Firmware Developer

NEW CAREER OPPORTUNITY




Build your career with Kraken Robotics. Make a difference to others. If innovation drives you, and you want a career that makes a difference to the world, helping humanity overcome the challenges in our oceans – safely, efficiently, and sustainably – then come join us at Kraken Robotics.




Kraken Robotics is currently recruiting for a Senior Firmware Developer to join our Mount Pearl, NL office, Dartmouth, Nova Scotia office or remote may be considered. 




We are seeking a skilled and motivated Senior Firmware Developer to join our software team focused on the development of our Towed Systems. In this role, you will be responsible for designing, implementing, and maintaining embedded firmware for sensor integration, power management, and communication interfaces used on these marine sensor platforms. 

 

ROLES AND RESPONSIBILITIES 

Design, develop, and debug embedded firmware for microcontrollers and real-time operating systems (RTOS). 
Develop and optimize firmware for real-time control, navigation, telemetry, and data logging systems. 
Implement robust communication protocols (CAN, RS-485, Ethernet, UART, SPI, I2C, etc.) for subsea environments. 
Ensure firmware reliability under extreme underwater conditions including pressure, temperature, and EMI. 
Collaborate with cross-functional teams (mechanical, electrical, and software) to support sys

title: Senior Firmware Developer, SeaPower/Senior-Firmware-Entwickler, SeaPower

Senior Firmware Developer, SeaPower/Senior-Firmware-Entwickler, SeaPower

Senior Firmware Developer, SeaPower/Senior-Firmware-Entwickler, SeaPower

NEW CAREER OPPORTUNITY




Build your career with Kraken Robotics. Make a difference to others. If innovation drives you, and you want a career that makes a difference to the world, helping humanity overcome the challenges in our oceans – safely, efficiently, and sustainably – then come join us at Kraken Robotics.

 

Kraken Robotics is currently seeking a Senior Firmware Developer to join our team in Rostock, Germany or Dartmouth, NS.




The Senior Firmware Developer will work on the SeaPower product line, delivering high-reliability energy storage and power management systems for subsea and marine environments. In this role, you will develop embedded firmware for Battery Management Systems (BMS) and Energy Storage Systems (ESS) used in mission-critical applications. 




As a senior contributor, you will take ownership of complex firmware components, influence architecture and development practices, and work closely with electrical, mechanical, systems, software, QA, and production teams to ensure robust, well tested, production ready solutions across the full product lifecycle.

Design, develop, and debug bare metal embedded firmware for microcontrollers.
Mentor early career firmware developers through regular code reviews, design guidance, and techni

Salary: $82,016 - $106,621 per annum (HL-23), 37.50 hours per week

Benefits: Onboarding

Position Details

Department of Social Supports and Well-Being

The Clinical Program Supervisor (CPS) is an integral leadership role within the Social Supports and Well-Being (SSWB) program areas and is suited to employees with strong organizational knowledge, clinical expertise, and experience working in complex service environments. The CPS provides leadership for programs delivered under the Children Youth and Families Act, Adoptions Act, Young Persons Offences Act, and the Youth Criminal Justice Act. This role includes clinical supervision of social workers, social worker assistants, community service workers, and administrative staff; oversight of SSWB service delivery; and leadership in case planning. description:

Compensation

OverviewApplication
About Us

Propel builds technology that strengthens the social safety net in America. We envision a future where every American has the tools and resources they need to thrive.

The safety net's strength depends not just on funding, but delivery. Propel exists to dramatically improve how government benefits are delivered. Managing SNAP benefits, picking a D-SNP plan, or protecting against EBT theft should feel as seamless as Apple Pay or Netflix.

Governments struggle to build modern, scalable user experiences. We don't.

Over 5.5 million Americans trust Propel today. We've grown revenue 35% YoY and are reinventing entire benefit categories, including Medicare, around the real needs of low-income Americans. We're building a high-growth, high-ceiling, sustainable for-profit company where impact drives revenue.

About the Role

We're hiring a part-time Office & People Operations Intern to help keep our Brooklyn office running and our team well-supported. This role sits at the intersection of people ops, facilities, and administrative coordination - the kind of work that makes everything else easier.

Our current office manager is moving on to a new opportunity, and we're restructuring the responsibilities of the team and the

title: Senior Brokerage Operations Associate
description:
Senior Brokerage Operations Associate
Location

USA; Brooklyn, NYC

Employment Type

Full time

Location Type

Remote

Department

New Ventures

OverviewApplication
About Us

Propel is on a mission to empower low-income Americans by simplifying access to government benefits with modern technology. We're a passionate team of 100 Propellers - all working to help our users get through the month, every month.

The Role

Propel owns Willoughby, a licensed entity building the first app-based digital Medicare brokerage, initially focused on dual Medicare-Medicaid users.

We’re looking for a Senior Associate to help build and scale the operational foundation of our business. This is a highly cross-functional, individual-contributor role focused on automation, operations, analytics, and partner execution.

You’ll work closely with Product, Engineering, Data, Compliance, and GTM to reduce manual work, improve reliability, and enable Propel to scale into new markets and partnerships.

The core of what you’ll do
Back Office Automation

Build and own internal-facing tools that automate core backoffice and post-enrollment workflows

Identify high-impact opportunities to reduce manual effort, improve accuracy, and increase operational efficiency

Partner with Product and Engineering to scope, ship, and iterate quickly on automation

Operations & Partnerships

Support and increasingly own key operational partner relationships, including FMOs

Manage carrier contracting and onboarding to enab

Help Shape One of Canada’s Fastest‑Growing Regions

Halifax Regional Municipality (HRM) is a vibrant, diverse, and forward‑looking municipality that serves as the economic, cultural, and educational hub of Atlantic Canada. Home to more than half a million residents across over 200 communities, the Halifax region combines urban energy, suburban growth, rural character, and over 400 km of coastline to create an exceptional quality of life. HRM is experiencing one of the most sustained periods of population and development growth in its history.

As the region evolves toward a future of greater density, mobility, and complexity, the importance of thoughtful, credible planning leadership has never been greater.

HRM is now seeking its next Executive Director, Planning and Development. This is one of the most influential planning leadership roles in Canada. Planning decisions being made today will have generational impact on housing affordability, mobility, infrastructure investment, env

Founded in 1976 in Balmoral, New Brunswick, A.L.P.A. Equipment Ltd. (A.L.P.A) is a proudly family-owned and operated company that has grown into a leading supplier of forestry and construction equipment across Atlantic Canada and Eastern Quebec. With full-service branches in Balmoral, Fredericton, Edmundston, Moncton (NB), and Truro (NS), and a 63,000 sq. ft. manufacturing facility in Eel River Crossing, A.L.P.A. is known for its engineering excellence and local innovation  - most notably the Canadian-made Landrich Track Harvester. Representing top brands like Ponsse, Hyundai, JCB, Rotobec, and Fuchs, and employing over 150 people, the company offers full-service support and operator training. A.L.P.A. continues to be recognized for its leadership, receiving major government investment and actively supporting workforce development and education initiatives.

The Directors Guild of Canada, Newfoundland and Labrador Council (NLDGC) is seeking a new Business Agent to support our members working in dynamic production environments. This is a unique opportunity to play a central role in strengthening and advancing the film and television industry in Newfoundland and Labrador. 

The Organization 

The Director’s Guild of Canada represents creative and logistical professionals working in the film, television, and digital media industries across the country. The NLDGC advocates for its members by negotiating and administering collective agreements, supporting safe and fair working conditions, and promoting a strong, competitive production environment within the province.

A workplace that’s invested in your success. A team that truly values one another. A culture that is built upon integrity, passion and mutual respect. Are you ready to join a team of talented IT experts and take your career to new heights? If so, IMP Solutions is right for you.

We are seeking a skilled and motivated Tier 3 Engineer to join our Service and Support team in Halifax, NS. This role is pivotal in designing and implementing internal systems, customer-facing hosted and cloud environments, and providing technical assistance to team members on system and network-related requests.

This is an exciting opportunity to join a talented and growing team that is committed to providing service excellence, and is passionate about being part of something bigger. Are you ready for your next big step? Join our team!

What You Will Do:

Design and implement network and infrastructure solutions (WAN/LAN, routers, firewalls, servers, virtualization, Azure).

Architect and support hosted and cloud environments tailored to client needs.

Develop and maintain disaster recovery and network documentation.

Provide IT support for internal systems and Microsoft technologies (Windows Server, Exchange, SQL, SharePoint, O365).

At KPMG in Canada, our people bring their unique perspectives to Canada’s most important challenges. Here, you can build momentum that reaches beyond our business, develop skills for the future, and take ownership of your career with support at every stage. Join a firm where your career can make a difference.

 

KPMG Corporate Finance Inc. is the Ontario Securities Commission (OSC) regulated entity through which KPMG LLP's investment banking services are provided in Canada. KPMG's Corporate Finance services are principally comprised of merger, acquisition and divestiture advisory and capital advisory services.  

 

We offer independent financial advisory and investment banking services to both private and public companies. Acting independently of financing sources, we provide clients with pure, objective advice, focusing on strategies that can help meet corporate objectives. We have an extensive international network of over 2,600 corporate finance professionals working from 160 offices in 86 countries thus providing KPMG Corporate Finance with a broad local reach and established contacts.

 Job Posts Senior Technical Systems Analyst

SENIOR TECHNICAL SYSTEMS ANALYST

What is the opportunity?
We are seeking a Senior Technical System Analyst to join RBC Capital Markets’ Shared Services Analytics team. This role is central to designing, developing, and supporting business intelligence solutions that deliver critical reporting and analytics across the organization. You will lead technical initiatives including system upgrades, application migrations, and the development of data models and dashboards using industry-leading platforms. Working collaboratively with development teams, project managers, and business stakeholders, you will transform complex data requirements into scalable technical solutions that drive operational effectiveness and support strategic decision-making.

What will you do?

Design and develop business intelligence solutions, including technical architecture, system specifications, and data models using tools such as Tableau and Power BI
Leverage AI tools to migrate legacy data models and reports to strategic solutions
Lead planning and implementation of application deployments, system upgrades, and migrations while ensuring alignment with technology standards and best practices
Perform hands-on technical development and provide ongoing support for multiple reporting platforms and applications serving the Capital Markets business
Collaborate with business system

 Job Posts Background Director, Identity Data And Workflow Engineering (Global Security)

BACKGROUND DIRECTOR, IDENTITY DATA AND WORKFLOW ENGINEERING (GLOBAL SECURITY)

What is the opportunity?
The Director of Identity Data & Workflow Engineering leads the engineering strategy and delivery of identity data services, workflow automation, and cross-product IAM APIs across the Enterprise Identity and Access Management (EIAM) ecosystem. This role owns the engineering capabilities that power identity request workflows, privileged account inventory, global directory services, and the API layer that enables consistent, secure access to IAM capabilities across the enterprise.

Identity data and workflow engineering is foundational to delivering secure, efficient, and scalable identity services. The quality and accessibility of identity data — and the workflows and APIs that act on it — directly determine how quickly the organization can onboard users, detect risk, and respond to regulatory demands. This leader will build and operate the platforms that turn identity data into a strategic asset and provide a trusted foundation for downstream consumers including controls, reporting, governance, and emerging AI capabilities.

What will you do?

Solution Delivery, development, engineering and integration
Own the engineering roadmap and delivery for IAM workflow platforms, privileged account inventory, glob

FULL STACK DEV (JAVA/ANGULAR)

About the role
We are seeking a Full Stack Dev (Java/Angular) with 6 to 9 years of experience to join our dynamic team. The ideal candidate will have expertise in Angular JS AWS SQL Spring Boot Java JavaScript and mySQL. Experience in the Cards & Payments domain is a plus. This hybrid role offers the opportunity to work on cutting-edge projects that drive our company’s success and impact the financial industry positively.

In this role, you will:

Develop and maintain high-quality software solutions using Angular JS AWS SQL Spring Boot Java JavaScript and mySQL to meet business requirements.
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to design implement and test software applications that enhance user experience and operational efficiency.
Ensure the scalability and performance of applications by optimizing code and implementing best practices in software development.
Participate in code reviews to maintain code quality and share knowledge with team members fostering a culture of continuous improvement.
Troubleshoot and resolve complex technical issues ensuring minimal disruption to business operations and maintaining high system availability.
Contribute to the design and architecture of software systems ensuring alignment with business goals and industry standards.
