 Job Posts IT Support Specialist

IT SUPPORT SPECIALIST

Halifax Public Libraries invites  INTERNAL and EXTERNAL applications for the full-time, permanent position of IT Support Specialist within the Information Technology Department located at Halifax North Memorial Public Library.

This is a unionized position. Preference will be given to internal NSUPE Local 14 employees.

Halifax Public Libraries is an inclusive employer committed to employment equity and building a diverse workforce. We encourage all qualified persons to apply and self-identify. We will accommodate applicants’ needs under the Nova Scotia Human Rights Act throughout all stages of the recruitment and selection process. If you require accommodation, we will confidentially address your needs.

ABOUT THE DEPARTMENT
The Information Technology Department works closely with library branches and departments to provide relevant, reliable and timely service. We support access to the library collection through the Integrated Library System, and provide public and staff access to PCs, laptops, iMacs, iPads, WiFi, printing, and gaming. We support productivity software, educational and entertainment applications.

KEY RESPONSIBILITES 

As an IT Support Specialist, at Halifax Public Libraries, you will:
Manage Microsoft 365 ecosystem and ensure all mobile and desktop devices are secure and optimized through MDM solutions.
Provide support

 Job Posts Technical Writer

TECHNICAL WRITER

Global Technology Solutions (GTS) at ResMed is a division dedicated to creating innovative, scalable, and secure platforms and services for patients, providers, and people across ResMed. The primary goal of GTS is to accelerate well-being and growth by transforming the core, enabling patient, people, and partner outcomes, and building future-ready operations.

The strategy of GTS focuses on aligning goals and promoting collaboration across all organizational areas. This includes fostering shared ownership, developing flexible platforms that can easily scale to meet global demands, and implementing global standards for key processes to ensure efficiency and consistency.

At Resmed, we are changing lives with every breath — improving patient quality of life, preventing chronic disease progression, and reducing healthcare system costs.  Our mission is to delight Resmed’s developer community with an opinionated yet empathetic developer experience that avoids surprise, is a joy to use, and abstracts the complexities of modern development to accelerate the pace of Resmed’s next-generation Healthcare Informatics platform.

Let’s talk about the Role

We’re looking for a curious, detail-driven Technical Writer to support our Developer Experience Platform team—the driving force behind building platform products that provide world- class developer experience

 Job Posts Sr Security Engineer

SR SECURITY ENGINEER

The Information Technology (IT) team plays a key role in providing business enablement throughout ResMed. We are focused on application, infrastructure, and user productivity solutions, with innovation, efficiency and security. Our goal is providing customer oriented agile delivery, effective business partnership and state-of-the-art technology solutions.

A key role of the Senior Security Engineer is to design, implement, and maintain Microsoft 365 security and compliance solutions, including Microsoft Purview, Information Protection, and DLP. LEITP Team Lead (IT Team Lead & Operations Manager)

Selection Process Nº: 239347-6

Salary: 121,918.00 USD per annum, plus benefits

Contract Type: Indeterminate (Full-time)

Term Details: This position is a Locally Engaged Staff position, subject to the Terms and Conditions of Employment for LES in USA

Job Function: Officer

Classification: LE - O2

Location: Peterson Space Force Base, Colorado Springs, Colorado

Number of Vacancies: 1

Closing date for application: 14 June, 2026 at 23:59 UTC/GMT -7:00

Apply Here

SUMMARY OF DUTIES

Our organization is seeking a qualified and motivated individual to provide Information Management and Technology (IM/IT) support at Peterson Space Force Base, located in Colorado Springs, Colorado. 

The incumbent will need to be intelligent, articulate and a hard-working individual with a high energy level and outstanding interpersonal skills.  The successful candidate will be a highly collaborative and detail-oriented professional who takes active roles in both day-to-day functions as well as within a team.  

Under the supervision of the Program Manager (MCO) or FSITP, the incumbent will:

Under the supervision of the deputy Head of Cooperation at the Embassy of Canada in Haiti and in close collaboration with the development team at Global Affairs Canada (GAC) in Ottawa, the Senior Development Officer supports all phases of international assistance programming and project management in Haiti. title: Growth & Insights Lead, Public Sector
description:
Growth & Insights Lead, Public Sector
Location

USA

Employment Type

Full time

Location Type

Remote

Department

Public Partnerships and Policy

Compensation
Base Salary $150K – $180K • Offers Equity
OverviewApplication

About Us

Propel is on a mission to empower low-income Americans by simplifying access to government benefits with modern technology. We're a passionate team of 100 Propellers - all working to help our users get through the month, every month.

The Role

Millions of Americans navigate a social safety net that too often works against them - confusing enrollment, broken eligibility systems, policies that never reach the people they're meant to serve. Propel exists to change that. And increasingly, the path to change runs through the states that administer these programs.

When Propel builds credible relationships with state agencies, we don't just grow the business - we create direct pathways to improving life for the people we serve. A state that understands how its users are falling through the cracks can actually fix it. But that requires state leaders to know what Propel sees and trust the source.

The Growth & Insights Lead will make that possible by reaching the right agency decision-makers, producing insights grounded in real user experience, and developing messaging that earns trust in a complex public sector environment. This is a full-cycle role: the right person will generate the idea, pull the data, and take it all the way to market themselves. S

title: Senior Field Service Representative
description:
Welcome to the Kraken Robotics Career Center

Senior Field Service Representative

Senior Field Service Representative

NEW CAREER OPPORTUNITY




Build your career with Kraken Robotics. Make a difference to others. If innovation drives you, and you want a career that makes a difference to the world, helping humanity overcome the challenges in our oceans – safely, efficiently, and sustainably – then come join us at Kraken Robotics.

 

Kraken Robotics is currently recruiting for a Senior Field Service Representative at our office in Mount Pearl, NL. 




The Senior Field Service Representative, reporting to the Service Manager, will build comprehensive knowledge across Krakens’ product line to provide support in the operation, maintenance and repair of Kraken products. This will be a Hybrid position split between office/workshop responsibilities and Field Service engagements. Approximately 50% Field based/50% office based including a mix of Offshore & Nearshore Field Service engagements.

Roles and Responsibilities

Field responsibilities include:

Field Service Calls - Leading field teams in the execution of field support engagements, both scheduled planned maintenance activities and corrective unplanned
Product Demonstrations - execution of product demonstrations, including internal initiatives and customer-led demonstrations such as Naval exercises. Includes installation, operation and maintenance of equipment, execution of mission objectives, reporting.
Operations Support

Do you have an interest in business operations and HR? Do you have a natural ability to build a collaborative and innovative team? Are you a Human Resource Professional who gets their energy from others?

We are seeking an Office Manager to join our leadership team supporting our people's experiences in our St. John's office for a full-time permanent position. Working closely with the Office Lead Partner for St. John's, this position is responsible for engaging and supporting high-performing teams through coaching, mentoring, and development.

Responsibilities

The Office Manager strives to promote office engagement through communication and relationship building. They are actively involved in the recruitment, onboarding, and ongoing learning & development of team members. The Office Manager will also provide support and leadership in special projects and/or key programs led by our People & Culture team. Fostering a culture of continuous improvement the Office Manager will support their office through change initiatives ensuring open communication and collaboration. Are you passionate about real estate and thrive in a dynamic, fast-paced setting? Do you enjoy supporting clients in achieving their dreams while managing multiple competing priorities?

Our Real Property team is seeking an experienced Property Paralegal to join our Sydney office for a full-time permanent position. As part of the Real Property team, you will support the real property lawyers, ensuring the needs of our clients are exceeded with every interaction.

As a Property Paralegal, you will be responsible for the following:

Assisting in all aspects of real estate transactions including residential, commercial, new construction, financings and subdivision property files;
Reviewing and drafting closing documents, including resolutions, officers' certificates, and security documents for financings;
Preparing closing books, drafting reporting letters and opinions;
Drafting various conveyance documents and associated closing documents including, Deed Transfer Affidavits, HST certificates, adjustments, and statutory declarations;
Conducting all accounting-related processes for all property transactions, including liaising with financial institutions to obtain mortg

Benefits: Onboarding

Position Details

Department of Health and Community Services

Reporting to the Senior Manager of Health Workforce Planning, the Manager plays a critical role in the development and implementation of comprehensive workforce strategies that support a sustainable, well-distributed, and highly skilled health workforce across the province in partnership with NL Health Services and other stakeholders. The Manager is responsible for workforce data through the development, maintenance, and continuous improvement of a provincial minimum data set (MDS), enabling consistent, high quality health workforce data collection ensuring that workforce information is accurate, timely, and suitable for advanced analytics, modeling, and decision making. The Manager works within the strategic and operational plans of the Department of Health and Community Services to develop recommendations to address key workforce issues and trends. The Manager collaborates closely with other government department

• gjøre CMSet til et effektivt arbeidsverktøy for kommunikasjonsavdelinga og resten av Kartverkets fagredaktører

• forberede innholdet for bruk gjennom API-

Avd. Brukeropplevelse og datadeling (BOD) i Digitaliseringsdirektoratet (Digdir) har behov for 1 kommunikasjonsrådgiver/-konsulent i ca 50 % til SAMT-BU (Sammenhengende tjenester for barn og unge).

SAMT-BU er et samarbeidsprosjekt om datadrevet tjenesteutvikling og sammenhengende tjenester for barn og unge – fra barnehage til høyere utdanning.

Prosjektets fulle tittel er Felles løft: Sammenhengende tjenester for barn og unge.

Felles Løft: Sammenhengende tjenester for barn og unge (SAMT BU) jobber for at barn, unge, foreldre og ansatte skal få bedre og mer helhetlige tjenester. Gjennom SAMT BU bygger vi et felles grunnlag for samarbeid på tvers av sektorer – med økt kunnskap om datadrevet tjenesteutvikling og sømløse brukerreiser. title: Senior/Experienced Product Designer
description:
Privacy Policy
Job Openings
Senior/Experienced Product Designer
R&D - Remote

What we’re doing
We’re building the AI powered coaching platform that transforms how gamers learn, compete, and win. Backed by a16z, we’re already live in 5 titles with 100K+ users and rapidly expanding into new games, hardware partnerships, and SDK integrations. 

The design team plays a significant role at trophi.ai. We shape how players first understand and feel the product, from the very first minute in the game through the loops that bring them back. We turn ambiguity into clear, intuitive experiences, use research and data to guide decisions, and partner closely with product, engineering, and data science to ship quickly. As a senior designer you will set the quality bar, own our systems and patterns, and define the look and feel of an AI powered coach that meets players inside their favorite games and our studio customers.




Things you’ll do

Lead end-to-end product design from discovery and flows to polished prototypes and user testing.
Turn ambiguous ideas into cohesive UX that drives core business outcomes around activation, retention and onboarding (First session clarity, engagement loops etc)
Partner with product and data science to run lightweight usability testing, interpret activation and retention funnels, and use insights to iterate quickly.
Obsess about impact, first on onboarding, first session clarity, and engagement loops.
Build and own our design system for speed and consiste

What we’re doing

title: Strategy & Operations Associate
description:
Privacy Policy
Job Openings
Strategy & Operations Associate
R&D - Toronto, Ontario (Hybrid)

What we’re doing
We’re building the AI-powered coaching platform that transforms how gamers learn, compete, and win. Backed by a16z with $12M raised, we’re live in 5 titles with 100K+ users and rapidly expanding into new games, hardware partnerships, and SDK integrations.




We’re hiring a Business Operations Associate to join our core team and work directly with the founders to build the backbone of our business. This is a unique opportunity to help scale the world’s first AI coaching platform for gamers at the intersection of gaming and AI.




What You’ll Do




Strategy & Analysis

Partner with the Head of Product & CEO on core strategic initiatives (B2B + B2C).
Lead market analysis, competitive deep dives, and opportunity sizing to identify trends, customer needs, and emerging opportunities.
Build business cases and quantitative models that inform go/no-go decisions on new games, partnerships, and investment bets.
Help chase down ambiguous, zero-to-one problems , from hypothesis to framework to action plan.



Ops & Execution

Build the operating backbone: architect workflows, OKRs, and business review processes that scale with the company.
Drive cross-functional initiatives across Product, Partnerships, and Marketing to ensure alignment and delivery.
Create leverage for leadership by taking point on pitch decks, proposals, and high-stakes projects that require polish and speed.

We’re building the AI-powered coaching platform that transforms how gamers learn, compete, and win. Backed by a16z and $12M in funding, we’re live in 5 titles with 100K+ users, expanding rapidly into new games, hardware partnerships, and SDK integrations.




You’ll Thrive Here If…

title: Intermediate Product Designer
description:
Privacy Policy
Job Openings
Intermediate Product Designer
R&D - St. John's, Newfoundland and Labrador

What we’re doing

We’re building the AI powered coaching platform that transforms how gamers learn, compete, and win. Backed by a16z, we’re already live in 5 titles with 100K+ users and rapidly expanding into new games and beyond our own app to power coaching for major partners in fitness, wearables, AR/VR, and even aviation. 




The design team plays a significant role at trophi.ai. We don't just build our own interface, we design "plug and play" coaching experiences that live inside our partners' ecosystems.




As a Product Designer, you will work hands-on to turn ambiguity into clear, intuitive experiences. You will partner closely with product and engineering teams to ship features quickly. Instead of managing others, your focus will be on craft: execute high-quality flows that might live in a mobile app, a post-session report, or an overlay in a simulator and designs that help users master complex skills, from landing a plane to perfecting a golf swing.




Things you’ll do

Execute end-to-end design and take projects from requirements and rough sketches to polished prototypes, embeddable UI components and SDKs.
Create cohesive UX flows that drive core business outcomes, specifically focusing on activation, onboarding, and first-session clarity.
Collaborate on research and
support product and data science teams in running usability tests, interpreting funnels, and usi

HR Officer

Venor has partnered with the College of Physicians & Surgeons of Nova Scotia (CPSNS) to recruit an HR Officer for a 21-month contract.

CPSNS serves and protects the public by effectively regulating the province’s medical profession in accordance with the Medical Act and its regulations. CPSNS is a culture of listening – listening to the public and the profession. The College prides itself in ensuring an environment that is respectful, safe and humble. CPSNS values engaging with Nova Scotians and the communities served across the province. Serving the public, the College ensures Nova Scotia’s physicians and surgeons are qualified, competent and fit to practice medicine to provide the best care possible to patients.

As an HR Officer, you will play a key role in supporting the full employee lifecycle while ensuring the effective delivery of payroll, benefits, and human resources programs. This position is integral to maintaining accurate and compliant HR operations, from compensation and benefits administration to recruitment, onboarding, and employee support. Job Title: General Manager  

Location: Wabush, NL 

Venor is thrilled to partner with our client, Industrial Rubber Company (IRC) in their search for a General Manager. IRC supplies critical rubber lining and piping solutions to the mining industry across Eastern Canada. With shops in Bathurst, NB and Wabush, NL, they are trusted by major mining operations for their expertise, quality, and fast turnaround. 

Position Overview 

We are seeking an experienced and motivated General Manager to lead the daily operations of their rubber lining shop. This role requires a hands-on leader who can effectively manage employees, oversee projects, maintain customer relationships, and drive business growth. The General Manager will be responsible for ensuring efficiency, quality, and profitability while upholding company standards for safety and service. 

What you’ll be doing: 

Operational Leadership: 

Plan, schedule, and oversee employee shifts and work assignments. 
Ensure projects are completed on time, within budget, and to quality standards. 
Monitor shop performance and implement process improvements.

title: Youth Peer Support Worker
description:
Privacy Policy
Job Openings
Youth Peer Support Worker
Youth Mental Health Program - St. John's, Newfoundland and Labrador

Association for New Canadians is a non-profit, community-based organization dedicated to the provision of settlement and integration services for immigrants and refugees. 

 

Job Title: Youth Peer Support Worker 

Location: St. John’s, NL 

Position Type: 10 Temporary Part-time positions 

Closing date: Until Filled 

 

Reporting to the Provincial Training Facilitator, the Youth Peer Support Worker provides non-clinical, peer-based

support to youth accessing the program. Grounded in lived or living experience, the role focuses on building connection, trust, and hope through mentorship, relational engagement, and strengths‑based support. The Peer Support Worker walks alongside youth as they navigate challenges, develop skills, and access community resources. 

This position is non‑clinical in nature and does not involve counselling, therapy, crisis intervention, diagnosis, assessment, or case management. 

Hours & Location: 10 hours per week. Primarily based at an English as an Additional Language school, with some community or school‑based work. Hours are flexible. 

 

 

Roles and Responsibilities: 

 

Build safe, respectful, and trusting relationships with youth participants 
Provide one-on-one and/or group peer support through shared lived experience 
Offer emotional support, encouragement, and validation in a non-judgmental way 
Support youth in
